title: Pegasus launches Swift reboost mission
summary: A Pegasus XL rocket conducted a mission to reboost NASA's Swift astrophysics spacecraft, potentially marking its final flight. The launch was reported by SpaceNews.

title: Chinese satellite manufacturer Hongqing raises $191 million
summary: Hongqing Technology, a satellite manufacturing affiliate of Chinese launch firm Landspace, raised $191 million in a funding round. This is one of the largest single raises for a Chinese commercial satellite manufacturer.

title: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies shifts focus to space
summary: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies is shifting its focus from rooftop applications to space, aiming to power orbital data centers and large satellite constellations with its thin-film solar technology.

title: Isar Aerospace to launch German-built Planet imaging satellite
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a contract with Planet’s German subsidiary to launch an imaging satellite, showcasing Germany's end-to-end space capabilities. This marks a significant milestone for both companies in the European space sector.

title: Polish space tech company Sybilla Technologies secures funds to enter U.S. market
summary: Sybilla Technologies, a Polish space tech company, has secured a $10 million investment from BGK and 3TS Capital Partners to expand into the U.S. market. The funding aims to support the company's growth and international presence.

title: Making satellite communications more resilient
summary: Kymeta's Ryan Stevenson discusses the benefits and challenges of multi-orbit and multi-band connectivity in a single terminal for satellite communications. The conversation highlights efforts to make satellite communications more resilient.

title: Final Atlas 5 Amazon Leo mission launches
summary: The final Atlas 5 rocket launched on July 2, carrying a payload of Amazon's Leo satellites. This mission marked the end of the Atlas 5's use for satellite launches.

title: Report links Starliner problems to overconfidence and unrealistic schedules
summary: A report attributes Boeing's Starliner technical issues to overconfidence, unrealistic schedules, and NASA's limited oversight. The CST-100 Starliner has faced persistent problems during its development.

title: FCC to vote on satellite licensing overhaul July 22
summary: The FCC will vote on July 22 to overhaul its satellite licensing process, aiming to streamline applications for large and complex satellite constellations. The changes are designed to create a more efficient 'licensing assembly line' to handle growing demand.

title: Unseen threats overhead: Drones endanger U.S. rocket launch sites
summary: Drones pose a significant threat to U.S. rocket launch sites, potentially causing millions in delays and affecting multiple launch providers. The issue highlights safety and operational risks in the space industry.

title: How to find Uranus this week, the hardest planet I've ever tried to see
summary: This week offers a rare opportunity to spot Uranus in the night sky, with Mars serving as a helpful guide. The event is ideal for amateur astronomers looking to enhance their skywatching skills.

title: Could humans someday explore Saturn's moon Titan, or will humanoid robots do it for us?
summary: The news discusses the potential for humans or AI-powered humanoid robots to explore Saturn's moon Titan in the future. It questions whether humans will set foot on Titan or if robots will undertake the journey instead.

title: 1 private spacecraft intercepts another on Space Force's groundbreaking 'Victus Haze' mission
summary: A U.S. Space Force mission, 'Victus Haze,' successfully completed its first tactical intercept of an orbital target using two satellites. This marks a groundbreaking achievement in space operations.

title: Celebrate 250 years of America with the Estes Liberty Star model rocket
summary: Estes released a limited-edition red, white, and blue model rocket to celebrate the 250th anniversary of America's Independence Day. The rocket is designed for both display and launch.

title: Space science has come a long way since July 4, 1776. Here's a look back at the saga
summary: Space.com reflects on the evolution of space science since 1776, highlighting key developments that transformed our understanding of space. The article ties this historical perspective to America's 250th birthday celebration.

title: Infant stars celebrate their independence with cosmic fireworks| Space photo of the day for July 3, 2026
summary: A new image from the James Webb Space Telescope shows infant stars displaying cosmic fireworks. This highlights the early stages of stellar formation and the telescope's advanced imaging capabilities.

title: America at 500: Where will we be in space in 2276?
summary: The article speculates on the future of space exploration in the year 2276, comparing the technological progress since the U.S. founding to potential advancements over the next 250 years. It reflects on humanity's progress from early flight to potential interstellar achievements.

title: NASA’s Hubble Captures Crimson Cloud Sparkling with White, Blue Stars
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a crimson gas cloud sparkling with blue and white stars. This observation highlights the telescope's ongoing contributions to astronomical science.

title: NASA’s Hubble Spots Star-Spangled Cosmic Scene
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the globular cluster Messier 3 (M3), showcasing over 500,000 stars in red, white, and blue hues. This cosmic scene highlights the vibrant and densely packed stellar population within the cluster.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Breaks Agency Streaming Record
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission achieved record-breaking viewership with over 149.4 million views across its live coverage, including launches, lunar flybys, and splashdowns. The mission demonstrated strong global public engagement through NASA's 24/7 streaming platforms.

title: Good Morning, Earth!
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured a photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. The ISS completes 16 orbits around Earth daily, experiencing 16 sunrises and sunsets.

title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Stars Sparking to Life in Cosmic Celebration
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured infrared images of stars forming in the FS Tau star system, revealing previously unseen details obscured by dust. The image also showcases numerous background galaxies, adding to the cosmic display.

title: What’s Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for July 2026, highlighting a Moon-and-planets alignment, a comet's return, Milky Way visibility, and Saturn's rings at a unique angle.

title: NASA’s Chandra Examines Milky Way at Arms’ Length
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory has revealed that the outer spiral arms of the Milky Way may be wider than previously believed, potentially altering astronomers' understanding of the galaxy's structure. The discovery was made through precise measurements of distances to dust clouds.

title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
summary: NASA is recruiting volunteers for a yearlong simulated mission to the Moon or Mars, starting in August 2027. The study will take place at Johnson Space Center to gather insights for future crewed deep space missions.

title: LINK Spacecraft Set for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: LINK Spacecraft, developed by Katalyst Space, is set to launch on July 2 to boost NASA's Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory into a higher orbit. This marks the first mission of its kind aimed at servicing an existing NASA satellite.

title: NASA’s Webb Studies How Planet Survived Death of its Star
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope is providing new insights into how a planet survived the death of its star. The observations offer clues about the far-future fate of solar systems like our own.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress to meet this timeline.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar missions and establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that severely damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company is prioritizing repairs to resume operations as soon as possible.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A mission to rescue the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final Atlas satellite launch. Several orbital launches are scheduled worldwide this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to launch the Swift Boost Mission to extend the life of a NASA space telescope. This will be the rocket's first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, providing data to support future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The image highlights the telescope's capabilities in studying the galaxy's dense core region.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Meanwhile, Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new modifications.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about the shuttle's history and contributions.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space industry news highlights six orbital launches worldwide, including the first Pegasus XL launch. The manifest also features Falcon 9 and Chinese missions.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle on Tuesday morning. The test was launched aboard a Falcon 9 rocket.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting. These events aim to inform industry about upcoming activities, priorities, and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm is being developed for space applications, aiming to enhance satellite servicing and maintenance. This innovation could significantly reduce costs and extend the lifespan of orbital assets.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and selecting materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these young minds to support spacecraft assembly.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust and reliable space technology, though specific details are not provided.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have led to stronger space industry collaborations with Middle Eastern companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for partnerships in the global space sector.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly successfully captured and delivered imagery of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving an order from SSC. The mission was completed ahead of the 72-hour deadline.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space for its second military satellite, Aurora. The mission is part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The company aims to leverage lunar dust for building support structures and shielding reactor cores.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA commended Blue Origin's progress in addressing past mishaps and revealed plans to award contracts for moon base missions. The agency aims to achieve monthly lunar landings using updated lander designs.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on landers heading to the Moon. It serves as a guide to these upcoming lunar missions.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic has partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. This collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion for the company into the 'space applications' sector.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from Esrange Space Center in Sweden by 2028 under a new franchise model. The collaboration with SSC Space marks Firefly's expansion into international markets.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's LEO constellation has achieved enough satellites in orbit to support initial service after a ULA launch. The milestone enables continuous service across initial latitudes.

title: Isar Aerospace Lands Big Win With Planet Launch Contract
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a launch contract with Planet to launch one of Planet’s next-generation Pelican satellites, potentially as early as late 2026. This marks a significant milestone for the German launcher currently in development.

title: Mitsubishi Electric to Acquire Infostellar
summary: Mitsubishi Electric has announced its intention to acquire Infostellar, a prominent company in the Japanese space sector. The acquisition aims to strengthen Mitsubishi Electric's position in the industry.

title: Vodafone Ireland Conducts Emergency Services D2D Call With AST SpaceMobile JV
summary: Vodafone Ireland successfully conducted a test call with AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation for emergency services connectivity. This test is part of efforts to enhance connectivity for first responders.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the growing importance of satellite networks for non-government applications and the types of apps dominating satellite traffic as thousands of new satellites are launched into orbit. It highlights the relevance of satellite internet beyond traditional uses.

title: Dish Satellite TV and Wireless Businesses File for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Dish Network's satellite TV and wireless businesses filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy to facilitate the transition of its Dish Wireless operations following spectrum transactions. The move aims to restructure the company's financial obligations and operational focus.

title: Ovzon Wins New Defense Deal with European NATO Nation
summary: Ovzon secured a $7.1 million defense contract with a European NATO nation for its satellite communications solution. The deal is valued at 74 million Swedish krona.

title: Unseenlabs Orders 2 Cubesats from GomSpace
summary: French radio frequency detection company Unseenlabs ordered two additional cubesats from GomSpace for 2.2 million euros ($2.5 million). The contract was announced by GomSpace on Wednesday.

title: Lite Coms Hires Calspan Exec Greg Campbell as President
summary: Lite Coms has appointed Greg Campbell, a former Calspan executive, as its new president. Campbell will lead the company's daily operations and focus on expanding manufacturing capabilities and investments.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines have been awarded NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of efforts to establish a lunar base. This marks a significant step in NASA's lunar exploration and commercial partnerships.
